data.description:
## Why

Audit OSS finding #2 (MEDIUM). The GitHub mirror has no CodeQL / SAST workflow. Security scanning today is gitleaks-config-driven secret detection on the Gerrit review path plus a gating pip-audit — good, but no static code analysis. CodeQL is free for public repos, runs fine on a read-only mirror (it only needs push/schedule triggers and writes results to the Security tab, not the tree), and adds an independent detection layer the audit's manual review cannot replicate continuously.

## What / files

Add .github/workflows/codeql.yml: analyze python (src/, scripts/) and javascript (the workflow editor assets), triggers on push to main + a weekly schedule, least-privilege permissions (security-events: write, contents: read), concurrency cancel-in-progress, actions SHA-pinned with tag comments to match the repo convention. Ensure alerts route somewhere watched (default Security tab; note in SECURITY.md if maintainer triage expectations change).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] .github/workflows/codeql.yml exists, runs green on main for both languages
- [ ] Permissions block is least-privilege; actions are SHA-pinned with version comments
- [ ] Weekly scheduled run configured in addition to push
- [ ] Any true-positive findings from the first run are triaged (fixed or dismissed with a reason) before the ticket closes
data.title: Add a CodeQL workflow on the GitHub mirror (python + javascript)
